herbs.herb_desc: Allium sativum, commonly known as garlic, is often used in the form of a syrup to provide various health benefits, including its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The syrup is typically made by infusing garlic in a solvent, such as glycerin or water, and can be used to support immune function and reduce inflammation. As a dietary supplement, allium sativum syrup may be used to promote cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues.
